title: Moricandia meyeri
intro: Moricandia meyeri, also known as Persian or Turkish cornflower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including reducing inflammation and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Moricandia meyeri include isothiocyanates, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Moricandia meyeri are teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat coughs, colds, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Moricandia meyeri is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. It is a type of annual plant native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia. The exact classification of Moricandia meyeri at the rank of genus or higher is within the tribe Alysseae. This classification helps distinguish it from other related plant species within the Brassicaceae family. Further classification may include its rank within the tribe, which is still a subject of ongoing research in botany.
